sections.heading: Termite & WDI Inspection in Northampton, MA
sections.meta: Standalone: $125 · As add-on: $95 with home inspection · NPMA-33 form delivered promptly

sections.heading: What Northampton Buyers and Owners Need to Know

blocks.html: Northampton's century-plus housing stock gives termites and powderpost beetles decades of access to original framing. Downtown Victorians regularly show old powderpost galleries in basement joists and sill rot at fieldstone foundations. Mill River-adjacent properties stay damp enough to keep subterranean termite pressure active. Florence ranches with mulched foundations and woodpiles get flagged for conducive conditions consistently.

Ken Arnold has been performing termite & wdi inspection services across the Pioneer Valley since 2004 — including hundreds of Northampton-area properties. Every inspection is performed personally by Ken (MA License #587), follows ASHI standards, and is delivered as a comprehensive photo-documented digital report.
NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.
For full scope, methodology, and pricing details, see the canonical service page: Termite & WDI Inspection. For broader context on Northampton's housing stock, see the Northampton town profile.

items.q: What's WDI and how is it different from termite-only?
items.a: WDI stands for Wood-Destroying Insect. The NPMA-33 form covers all wood-destroying insects relevant in our region: subterranean termites, carpenter ants, carpenter bees, and powderpost beetles. In the Pioneer Valley, carpenter ants are far more common findings than termites — but both are reported on the same form.

items.q: Is WDI inspection required for my mortgage?
items.a: Most VA loans require it. FHA loans require it in many states (and most lenders ask for it in Massachusetts as a matter of policy). Conventional loans don't typically require it but it's a common buyer-requested contingency. Confirm with your loan officer.

items.q: What if active activity is found?
items.a: Active activity gets disclosed on the NPMA-33 form. The remedy depends on the species and extent: localized carpenter ant treatment is usually under $500; full subterranean termite treatment in our region runs $1,200–$2,500 with annual monitoring. Buyer and seller typically negotiate treatment as part of the inspection contingency.

The form distinguishes 'evidence of past infestation' from 'evidence of active infestation.' Old galleries, frass, or exit holes without current insect activity are documented but generally don't trigger treatment requirements. Structural damage from old activity is a separate matter — that gets noted in the home inspection report and may need a structural assessment.

cards.title: Buyer Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: A thorough, independent inspection of every major system before you close — 22 years of Pioneer Valley experience documented in a detailed photo report.

cards.title: Pre-Listing Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Find out what a buyer's inspector will find — before you list. Get ahead of objections, price the home accurately, and shorten your time on market.

cards.title: New Construction Home Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: An independent inspection before your warranty closes. Phase, pre-drywall, or final-walk inspections — finding what the builder's punch list misses.

cards.title: Condo & Townhouse Inspection in Northampton
cards.body: Targeted inspection of your unit plus the shared-system review most condo buyers don't get — common to mill conversions, downtown buildings, and townhouse complexes across Western Mass.

cards.body: Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.
